effective_date.description: Effective date is the anchor for all obligation timelines and renewal monitoring; date accuracy is critical — even a one-day error can affect renewal notice windows

expiration_date.description: Expiration date drives renewal monitoring and auto-renewal notifications; must be extracted accurately to prevent inadvertent obligations

governing_law.description: Governing law determines which jurisdiction's rules apply to disputes, enforcement, and permissible confidentiality provisions; errors affect legal risk assessment

confidentiality_period_years.description: Confidentiality duration is a key obligation metric; inaccurate extraction could cause premature disclosure or over-restriction of information

agreement_type.description: Agreement type drives routing to the correct contract management workflow and reporting categories

party_1_name.description: Party identity is essential for counterparty risk management, OFAC screening, and vendor onboarding

party_2_name.description: Party identity is essential for counterparty risk management, OFAC screening, and vendor onboarding

auto_renew.description: Auto-renewal status directly affects whether a renewal notice must be sent; false negatives can result in unwanted contract extensions

arbitration_clause.description: Presence of arbitration clause determines dispute resolution path; errors affect litigation strategy

non_solicitation_clause.description: Non-solicitation status must be tracked for HR and talent acquisition compliance; errors create legal exposure

non_compete_clause.description: Non-compete obligations are legally sensitive; accuracy required to inform business development and hiring decisions

return_or_destroy_obligation.description: Return/destroy obligation triggers an actionable process upon contract termination; errors cause data governance failures
